text:
Neurology Clinic

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 09 November 2024

Dr Patel
South Thames NHS Trust
Neurology Outpatients, Riverside Health Centre, 14 Millbank Way, London SE11 5AA

Dear Dr Patel,

Jordan Evans, DOB: 14-07-2002, Hospital No: H629451 NHS No. 8472036915
Flat 3, 22 Brook Street, London, W1K 4HA

Reason for correspondence:
Confirmation of reasonable adjustment – quiet waiting room for upcoming appointment.

I am writing to confirm we have arranged a quiet waiting room reasonable adjustment for Jordan Evans’s neurology appointment. As the university esports club lead with prolonged screen time and irregular sleep, Jordan Evans reports sensitivity to busy, noisy environments which can exacerbate headaches, visual strain, and fatigue. To support timely assessment and comfort, we will:

- Offer check-in via reception fast-track and seating in a low-stimulation area.
- Minimise waiting time where feasible and provide dimmer lighting.
- Allow use of noise-reduction headphones and personal hydration.
- Provide written information to supplement verbal discussion.

If there are further sensory or scheduling preferences (e.g., first-morning slot), please advise in advance so we can accommodate.

Our bookings team will contact Jordan Evans 48 hours prior to the appointment to reconfirm arrangements. No changes to treatment have been made at this stage.

Yours sincerely,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: I reviewed the letter in full. It discusses a reasonable adjustment for a quiet waiting room due to sensitivity to busy environments, headaches, visual strain, and fatigue. There is no mention of seizures, epilepsy, or any seizure frequency or seizure-free interval. Therefore, there is no seizure frequency information to extract.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter confirming a quiet waiting room reasonable adjustment for an epilepsy appointment

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
